name: Baba Ganoush
tagline: Fire-roasted eggplant with tahini, lemon, garlic, smoke
highlight: Burnt-skin eggplant gives the haunting smoke; no eggs anywhere

hero.alt: Baba ganoush plated with olive oil pool and pomegranate
hero.caption: baba ganoush — smoky and lush

healthFacts: Charred-skin eggplant infuses smoke without an open fire.
Pomegranate seeds add tang and a hit of polyphenols.

steps.title: Char the eggplant
steps.detail: Pierce eggplants with a fork. Char directly over a gas flame or under a high broiler 12–15 min, turning, until skin is blackened and inside collapses.
steps.tip: Burnt skin = smoke. Don't be polite about the char.

steps.title: Drain pulp
steps.detail: Cool eggplants 10 min. Slit lengthwise, scoop pulp into a colander; let excess water drain 10 min.
steps.tip: Wet eggplant = watery dip. Don't rush this drain.

steps.title: Mash, don't blend
steps.detail: Roughly chop pulp on a board. Transfer to a bowl. Stir in tahini, lemon, grated garlic, salt — fork-mashed, not pureed.
steps.tip: Texture is the whole point. Blender = baby food.

steps.title: Pool oil and top
steps.detail: Spread on a plate, swirl a well, pool olive oil. Dust with smoked paprika, scatter pomegranate seeds and parsley.
steps.tip: Tastes better the next day — flavors deepen overnight.

media.alt: Baba ganoush plated with olive oil
media.caption: pool oil and top
